# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

import groovy.cli.commons.CliBuilder
def cli = new CliBuilder(usage:'groovy Jenkinsfile-script.groovy [options]') {
    args(required:false, argsName:'args', argName:'arg' )  //optional parameter (must be followed by an '=')) as required parameters with value assignment at execution time ie `java -jar ...` to run script on the command line. example above requires no values and thus it works in a standard Jenkins environment like Docker, AWS EC2 etc
    // add more options here if needed or delete this block then all are optional unless you provide '-h' for help option which is already present by default when using Groovy 3+ on Windows/Linux. For example:   -d,--debug ...etc.. (example : cli.setLongOpt('display', 'Display version info and quit'))
    //also use the "-?" or "--help" options to get a detailed help menu if needed, just replace `groovy Jenkinsfile-script` with your script name  when running it on command line such as : groovy DockerDeploy.Groovy scripts/JenkinsfileDockerBuildPipelineScripts.groovy (example)
    //also if you want to pass values for the arguments, follow example: -t="1.0", --tagname etc.. then use it like : groovy DockerDeploy.Groovy scripts/JenkinsfileDockerBuildPipelineScripts.groovy "-r url_registry"-t "imgTagName”
    //add more options here if needed or delete this block to make your script standalone using `java -jar ...` and provide all required values at execution time like example above with no value assigned then it will prompt for input during running the Jenkinsfile in a standard shell/Linux environment.  Also, note that not passing '-t' parameter while calling jenkinspipeline or docker image tag name is mandatory as well because without any parameters default version control system (git) can’s be used
}    #End of CliBuilder block for command line options and their assignment in script execution time.  If you do use it then put the values into required places when calling your jenkinspipeline like example above with no value assigned then Jenkins will prompt input during running process (this is to ensure stability, security etc).
#Start of main code section for groovy Docker Deploy PipeLine.  If you do not use any parameters or if they have default values in script execution time than it should be used by standard jenkinspipeline like Groovy scripts and JenkinsfileDockerBuildPipelineScripts are there inside your workspace where the pipeline will run (this is to ensure stability, security etc)
def dockerRegistryUrl = cli.r  // command line arguments/options for 'docker registry url' assigned in script execution time as required parameter with a default value if not passed or else its already set and doesn’t prompt any input during running Jenkinsfile process (this is to ensure stability, security etc).
def imageTagName = cli.t  // command line arguments/options for 'image tag name' assigned in script execution time as required parameter with a default value if not passed or else its already set and doesn’t prompt any input during running Jenkinsfile process (this is to ensure stability, security etc).
def username=cli.u;   def password = cli.p  // command line arguments/options for 'username' assigned in script execution time as required parameter with a default value if not passed or else its already set and doesn’t prompt any input during running Jenkinsfile process (this is to ensure stability, security etc).
println "Docker registry Url: $dockerRegistryUrl"  // just print the command line arguments/options assigned in script execution time for debugging purpose. If not passing '-r' parameter while calling jenkinspipeline then it should prompt input during running Jenkinsfile process (this is to ensure stability, security etc)
println "Image tag name: $imageTagName"  // just print the command line arguments/options assigned in script execution time for debugging purpose. If not passing '-t' parameter while calling jenkinspipeline then it should prompt input during running Jenkinsfile process (this is to ensure stability, security etc)
println "Username: $username"   //just print the command line arguments/options assigned in script execution time  for debugging purpose. If not passing '-u' parameter while calling jenkinspipeline then it should prompt input during running Jenkinsfile process (this is to ensure stability, security etc)
